title: rubus tayulinensis
intro: Rubus tayulinensis, also known as Tayuling raspberry, is a type of wild raspberry native to Taiwan. It is a shrub with edible fruits and leaves that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves of Rubus tayulinensis are believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as possessing antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, or its leaves can be dried and used as a dietary supplement in various herbal preparations.
